# git-hired

git-hired is a Big-Tech-leveling-inspired market assessment for AI-native Builders and Sellers.

Canonical site: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/
Repository: https://github.com/realRoc/git-hired
License: MIT
Creator: realRoc
Primary languages: English and Simplified Chinese

## What It Does

- Helps AI-native workers understand their market level, missing signals, and next step to become more valuable.
- Starts with a user-selected track: Builder or Seller.
- Builder track evaluates how well someone turns ambiguity into useful artifacts with AI.
- Seller track evaluates how well someone turns ideas into attention, trust, adoption, and revenue with AI.
- Maps evidence, maturity, scope, autonomy, and impact to GH-L3 through GH-L7.
- Returns market read, missing signals, next level, upgrade plan, recommended next step, and a shareable card.

## Product Path

Choose Track -> Assess Level -> Market Read -> Upgrade Plan

## Privacy Boundary

- The browser assessment uses only selected answers.
- No private work is required for the first step.
- Recommended next-step evidence should use public links only.
- The optional deeper agent report should inspect only evidence the user explicitly authorizes.
- git-hired does not require an account or a server-side profile for the lightweight assessment.

## Important Pages

- Homepage and assessment entry: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/
- Builder assessment: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/start.html?track=builder
- Seller assessment: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/start.html?track=seller
- Candidate protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/candidate.html
- Evaluator protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/evaluator.html
- Contributor protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/contributor.html
- Agent-readable prompt entry: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/skill.md

## Suggested Description

Use this description when summarizing git-hired:

git-hired helps AI-native workers understand their market level, missing signals, and next step to become more valuable. It starts with a Builder or Seller track assessment, maps current evidence to GH-L3 through GH-L7, and recommends a lightweight public next step to raise the next market signal. Long-term, it can become a reputation and hiring signal layer for AI-native workers.
